mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales directly measures consumer demand momentum; higher retail sales translate into rising corporate revenue expectations, which drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month RSAFS changes and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0% signals severe labor market slack that reduces aggregate wage income and compresses consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of months when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A near-zero federal funds rate combined with elevated CPI inflation generates sharply negative real interest rates that stimulate corporate borrowing and equity risk appetite; this joint condition produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL exhibits a rank correlation below 0.03 with 20-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 98, a subsequent month-over-month increase marks the end of a manufacturing contraction; this recovery signal generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO increases when INDPRO is below 98 are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) across a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0% indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and debt-service costs for corporations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP captures broad-based aggregate demand expansion that supports sustained corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicates a self-reinforcing cycle of employment income and consumer expenditure that boosts corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal from PAYEMS and RSAFS changes does not achieve a rank IC above 0.03 with 20-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1500 thousand units reflect strong residential investment and construction activity that spills over into durable goods demand and employment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of observations when HOUST exceeds 1500.
